title: Syzygium alcinae
intro: Syzygium alcinae, also known as the Syzygium alcinae tree, is a plant species belonging to the family Myrtaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help in alleviating conditions like fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Syzygium alcinae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Syzygium alcinae include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Syzygium alcinae is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae, commonly known as the myrtle family. It belongs to the genus Syzygium, which comprises over 1,200 species of plants, primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Syzygium alcinae is native to the tropical forests of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands in India. The plant is closely related to other species in the genus, such as Syzygium cumini and Syzygium jambos. Further classification may include its order (Myrtales) and kingdom (Plantae).
uses: Syzygium alcinae has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, but specific information about its uses is limited.
